sourceMovie.title: Bugonia
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Bugonia for the same reason it broke through in 2025: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Yorgos Lanthimos keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The science fiction and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Mars Attacks!
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Mars Attacks! shares Bugonia's gift for science-fiction — the 1996 film delivers comedy without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: Greenland
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Bugonia compelling — the science-fiction, the thriller — shows up in Greenland (2020) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that tense storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: Sunshine
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The science-fiction energy of Bugonia is alive in Sunshine (2007), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a tense payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: They Live
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: They Live (1988) operates in the same science-fiction as Bugonia, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The tense payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: Mickey 17
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Mickey 17 (2025) belongs to the same conversation as Bugonia — both anchor their science-fiction in comedy emotion that doesn't let go. The funny undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: Sorry to Bother You
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Bugonia's science-fiction stayed with you, Sorry to Bother You (2018) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The comedy through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Sleeper
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Bugonia excels in science-fiction, Sleeper (1973) doubles down on comedy to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: They Cloned Tyrone
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: They Cloned Tyrone (2023) is the natural next step after Bugonia: same science-fiction DNA, different perspective. The funny streak and comedy elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Repo Man
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Bugonia who respond to its science-fiction will find Repo Man (1984) immediately familiar. Both films share a comedy sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Valerian and the City of a Thousand Planets
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Bugonia fans keep landing on Valerian and the City of a Thousand Planets (2017) — the science-fiction is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

faq.question: What should I watch after Bugonia?
faq.answer: Try Mars Attacks!, Greenland, Sunshine—each shares DNA with Bugonia while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Bugonia?
faq.answer: Bugonia (2025) is best described as Science Fiction, Thriller, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Bugonia worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this science fiction lane.
